_doc: Operational state for Chief Decifer. Hand-curated. Keep short. Update when features are enabled/disabled, when a deliberate decision ships, or when a known tradeoff changes.

disabled_features.why: No IBKR FX market data subscription
disabled_features.implication_for_chief: Do not raise risks about FX execution, FX orphan stops, or FX lifecycle bugs. FX fixes in recent commits are cleanup of legacy state, not active exposure.

recent_decisions.decision: Direction Agreement Ratio (DAR) shipped; composite score cap removed
recent_decisions.context: Deliberate — discussed with Amit. Uncapped score is the intended new behavior.
recent_decisions.implication_for_chief: Do not classify the cap removal as a risk. If worth flagging at all, put it in observations framed as 'monitor score distribution post-change.'

recent_decisions.decision: Paper thresholds deliberately loose (min_score 18, agents_required 3, max_positions 20)
recent_decisions.context: Phase A design choice — cost of bad paper trade = 0, value = training data.
recent_decisions.implication_for_chief: Do not frame loose paper config as a risk. Live values preserved as inline comments in config.py.

recent_decisions.decision: HMM regime detection deferred; VIX-proxy + SPY EMA is production
recent_decisions.context: Locked until >=200 closed trades + IC Phase 2 review.
recent_decisions.implication_for_chief: Do not recommend turning on HMM. Do not flag 'only VIX-proxy is running' as a risk.

accepted_tradeoffs.reason: Tests and code diverged during rapid development. Runtime unaffected. Policy: don't spend sessions fixing unless directly related to the work being done.
accepted_tradeoffs.implication_for_chief: Low pass rate alone is not a risk. Only flag specific failing tests in code paths that are active and untested elsewhere.

accepted_tradeoffs.reason: Multiple orthogonal improvement tracks (sizing, regime, signals) proceed in parallel because they touch different modules.
accepted_tradeoffs.implication_for_chief: Do not flag WIP count alone as a risk. Only flag if two specific features are observed modifying the same function/section.
